title: 10 possible side effects of Jasminum sambac (arabian jasmine)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian jasmine, causes allergic reactions due to its high concentration of allergenic compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes. These substances can trigger an immune response in some individuals, leading to symptoms like hives, itching, and respiratory issues. The strong scent of the plant may also exacerbate these reactions, making it essential for users to be aware of their potential sensitivity to Jasminum sambac.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian jasmine, induces dizziness suddenly due to its ability to relax the muscles and slow down the heart rate. This can cause a temporary drop in blood pressure, leading to feelings of lightheadedness or dizziness. Additionally, the plant's natural sedative properties can interact with certain medications, exacerbating the dizziness.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian jasmine, triggers headache episodes due to its high content of volatile oils, particularly linalool and benzoic acid. These compounds can cause an allergic reaction or irritation in some individuals, leading to symptoms such as headaches, dizziness, and nausea. The intense aroma and fragrance of the plant may also be overwhelming for sensitive individuals, exacerbating existing headache conditions or triggering new episodes.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian Jasmine, produces nausea symptoms in some individuals. This is attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils, particularly benzyl benzoate and linalool, which can cause stomach discomfort and unease when ingested or used in aromatherapy. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids and alkaloids may also contribute to these adverse effects, potentially triggering nausea and gastrointestinal upset in susceptible individuals.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian Jasmine, increases heart rate rapidly due to its stimulatory properties on the nervous system. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, can cause an increase in adrenaline levels, leading to a rapid heartbeat. This effect is thought to be a result of the plant's ability to interact with neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which regulate heart rate and blood pressure. As a natural stimulant, Jasminum sambac may enhance alertness and energy, but it can also cause an adverse reaction in some individuals.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian Jasmine, causes insomnia problems. The essential oil extracted from its flowers can stimulate the brain and nervous system, leading to restlessness and difficulty falling asleep. This is due to the presence of linalool and benzyl acetate, which are known to have a stimulating effect on the central nervous system. As a result, individuals may experience vivid dreams, anxiety, and an increased sense of alertness, making it challenging to get a good night's sleep.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian jasmine, lowers blood pressure significantly due to its active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects. These compounds relax the blood vessels, reducing peripheral resistance and causing a decrease in blood pressure. Additionally, the herb's ability to inhibit angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) may also contribute to its hypotensive effects, further reinforcing its potential to lower blood pressure.

Jasminum sambac aggravates anxiety attacks by stimulating the nervous system and releasing excessive amounts of serotonin in the brain. This can lead to feelings of panic, unease, and apprehension. Additionally, the fragrance of Jasminum sambac can cause an overload on the senses, further exacerbating anxiety symptoms. As a result, individuals may experience increased heart rate, sweating, and trembling.

Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian Jasmine, worsens depression episodes. This is likely due to its high concentration of volatile oils, such as linalool and benzoic acid, which can have a profound impact on the nervous system. The plant's strong aroma and flavor compounds may disrupt the body's natural chemical balance, exacerbating symptoms of depression and potentially leading to increased feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and anxiety.

Jasminum sambac stimulates excessive sweating due to its ability to increase heart rate and body temperature. The plant's natural properties can cause an increase in blood circulation, which may lead to a sensation of warmth and sweating. Additionally, the aromatic compounds present in Jasminum sambac may also contribute to this side effect by stimulating the nervous system and releasing sweat glands.

intro: Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian Jasmine, has some side effects when used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and stomach upset. Causes of these issues include excessive consumption, poor quality extraction methods, or individual sensitivities. For instance, an allergic reaction can lead to discomfort, itching, and even anaphylaxis, significantly impacting daily activities and social interactions.
intro_desc: Jasminum sambac, also known as Arabian jasmine, has some side effects when used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. These side effects are often caused by allergic responses to the plant's sap or essential oils. For example, if a person has sensitive skin, touching the plant could lead to itchy rashes and redness, hindering their daily activities and social interactions. In severe cases, anaphylaxis could occur, requiring immediate medical attention.
